title: Image Size, Build Performance, Runtime Efficiency, and Reproducibility · Independent Container Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer image size, build performance, runtime efficiency, and reproducibility into a new measured build and runtime optimization experiment without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of measured build and runtime optimization experiment with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.
Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.
Compare trusted base, distroless or scratch, packages, debugging needs, certificates, locales, dynamic libraries, and patch workflow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Image Size Layer Analysis
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests. Competency probe: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Image Size Layer Analysis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Image Size Layer Analysis, keep Docker Image Size Layer Analysis connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Reproducible Build Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-reproducible-build-evidence-ueleil". Tailor its build-performance-budget.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Reproducible Build Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Reproducible Build Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Reproducible Build Evidence, keep Docker Reproducible Build Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-reproducible-build-evidence-ueleil
  - competency: docker-reproducible-build-evidence
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: reproducible build evidence
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rotate a required secret between build and runtime
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: ueleil
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
